title: Military victory, political stalemate: The US's strategic myopia in the Middle East
subhed: Two weeks after launching the war against Iran alongside Israel, the Trump administration appears not to have anticipated Iranian strikes against neighboring countries, the closure of the Strait of Hormuz or the organized succession within the Iranian regime. It is also becoming clear that the two allies do not share the same priorities.

title: After a soldier killed in Iraq, France confronts the costs of its Middle East engagement
subhed: Emmanuel Macron announced the death of a French service member in a drone strike claimed by a pro-Iranian armed group. Six other soldiers were wounded.

title: New war in Lebanon is price of international community's silence
subhed: Ever since Israel and Lebanon signed a theoretical truce in November 2024, which Israel never respected, the international community has stayed almost entirely silent, implicitly accepting the rule of force over international law.

title: Pritzker Prize honors Chilean Smiljan Radic, architect of interiority
subhed: Ten years after Alejandro Aravena, Radic has become the second Chilean to be awarded what is considered the Nobel Prize of architecture.

title: France's far right sets measured ambitions for local elections
subhed: While the Rassemblement National is fielding candidates in 34 municipalities with over 100,000 residents, its highest hopes lie with Perpignan – already under the party's control – Toulon and Nice. It is mainly focusing on medium-sized cities in the northeast and southeast of France, its two traditional strongholds.

Jeffrey Epstein's Parisian lives
subhed: Over 18 years, the extremely kitsch property at 22 Avenue Foch served Epstein as a European office, a salon for artists and scientists and, above all, one of the places where he committed his sexual crimes.

title: Pete Hegseth, the embodiment of Trump's war in Iran
subhed: The US secretary of defense, a former Fox News host, has ardently defended the operation against the Islamic Republic. He has disregarded rules of engagement and boasted about American supremacy.

title: The ghosts of the new Chilean president's family
subhed: José Antonio Kast, who will be sworn in on Wednesday, makes no secret of his admiration for Augusto Pinochet's regime. He is less forthcoming about his father's past as a former Wehrmacht soldier and his family's role during the atrocities committed in Paine during the 1973 coup.

'The Iranian people remain the first and last losers of the war in the Middle East'
Dominique Eddé, Lebanese writer: 'The present moment is overwhelming; it is one of destruction. All that remains is to think and to stand tall'
